channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A diminished Hezbollah is made even weaker by the toppling of Assad in Syria
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A severely hobbled Hezbollah was in no position to help defend former Syrian President Bashar Assad, a longtime ally, from the lightning-fast insurgency that toppled him. With Assad gone, the militant group based in Lebanon is even weaker.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrians cheer end of 50 years of Assad rule at first Friday prayers since government fell
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Exuberant Syrians observed the first Friday prayers since the ouster of President Bashar Assad, gathering in the capital's historic main mosque, its largest square and around the country to celebrate the end of half a century of authoritarian rule.
item.dc:creator: ALBERT AJI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US military flies missing American out of Syria
item.description: The U.S. military on Friday flew a missing American citizen out of Syria this week, after the stunning fall of Syrian leader Bashar Assad's regime. Travis Timmerman was transferred to U.S. forces in Syria on Friday, according to a defense official, and was flown to Jordan. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israel eyes Iran nuke sites amid reports Trump mulls moves to block Tehran atomic program
item.description: Israel is once again said to be eyeing strikes on Iran's nuclear program as the incoming Trump administration is reportedly mulling plans on how to come down hard on Iran without prompting a war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU nations agree a crackdown on migrant smugglers. Some fear it may target people trying to help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union countries agreed on Friday a draft law aimed at preventing and countering migrant smuggling, which critics say could be used to target people or charity groups that try to help migrants in trouble.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Macron names centrist ally Bayrou as France's next prime minister
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has named centrist ally François Bayrou as prime minister, after a historic parliamentary vote ousted the previous government last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew is barred from UK
item.description: A suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew has been barred from the United Kingdom because of concerns that he posed a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Who is Travis Timmerman, the missing American apparently found in Syria
item.description: An American man found wandering the streets outside the Syrian capital of Damascus barefoot identified himself as Pete Travis Timmerman on Thursday. Timmerman was last seen in Budapest, Hungary, in June and had not been heard from in months.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: China's Xi is likely to decline Trump's inauguration invitation, seeing it as too risky to attend
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chinese leader Xi Jinping would likely see President-elect Donald Trump’s invitation to attend his inauguration as too risky to accept, and the gesture from Trump may have little bearing on the increasingly competitive ties between the two nations as the White House changes hands,
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jake Sullivan says Netanyahu 'ready to do a deal' as Hamas said to concede to Israel cease-fire demands
item.description: Hamas and Israel have signaled they may be taking more serious steps to reach a cease-fire and to secure the release of the hostages held in Gaza for more than a year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trudeau declares himself ‘proud feminist’ after lamenting Harris loss to Trump as setback for women
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Tuesday declared himself a "proud feminist" as he lamented Vice President Harris’ loss to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zimbabwe's president once faced the death penalty and is now set to abolish it
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — Zimbabwe’s Senate has approved a bill to abolish the death penalty, a key step in scrapping a law last used in the southern African nation nearly 20 years ago. Zimbabwe's Parliament said Thursday that the bill was passed by senators the night before.
item.dc:creator: FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Paraguay reopens its embassy in Jerusalem, handing Israel a rare diplomatic victory
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The small South American nation of Paraguay reopened its embassy in Jerusalem on Thursday, becoming the latest of just a small handful of countries to recognize the bitterly contested city as Israel’s capital.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida Republican brings 'America First' bent to powerful Foreign Affairs Committee
item.description: Rep. Brian Mast’s (R-Fla.) surprising selection to be the next chair of the House Foreign Affairs Committee is the latest sign of the Republican Party being reshaped by the next generation of "America First," pro-Trump leaders.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: At least 110 people have died in 7 weeks of post-election protests in Mozambique, rights groups say
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — More than 100 people including children have been killed by security forces in post-election protests in Mozambique that have lasted nearly two months, rights and civil society groups said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks businessman Leandro Rizzuto Jr. to be ambassador to the Organization of American States
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Wednesday that he is tapping Leandro Rizzuto Jr., former Conair executive and consul to Bermuda, to be an ambassador for the Organization of American States.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: North Korea condemns South Korea as 'fascist dictatorship' after martial law scare
item.description: North Korean dictator Kim Jong Un denounced South Korea as a 'fascist dictatorship' following President Yoon Suk Yeol's failed attempt to impose martial law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Navy defeats Houthi attacks in Gulf of Aden for second time in weeks
item.description: U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale (DDG 106) and USS O’Kane (DDG 77) successfully defeated a range of Houthi-launched weapons while transiting the Gulf of Aden for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China’s sci-fi spherical Death Star-like robot cop uses AI, facial recognition to track criminals
item.description: Chinese robotics company, Logon Technology, unveiled the RT-G autonomous spherical robot which will use AI and facial recognition to aid law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1,500-year-old Ten Commandments tablet set for auction
item.description: The tablet has twenty lines of text but only nine of the commandments, with "Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord in vain" omitted.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: The U.S. has had troops in Syria for the last decade to battle IS. The tumult following a rebel offensive that toppled Assad has raised fears of an Islamic State resurgence.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US hostage envoy in Beirut to seek information on missing journalist Austin Tice
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government's top hostage negotiator is in Beirut in hopes of collecting information on the whereabouts of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for 12 years, the State Department said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: US will remain in eastern Syria and seek to prevent IS resurgence, Pentagon says
item.description: Hours after Syrian rebels announced they had toppled Bashar al-Assad's government, the U.S. deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East said the U.S. will remain in eastern Syria to prevent an ISIS resurgence.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian government falls in stunning end to 50-year rule of Assad family
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian government fell early Sunday in a stunning end to the 50-year rule of the Assad family after a sudden rebel offensive sprinted across government-held territory and entered the capital in 10 days.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ZEINA KARAM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump back on the world stage at Notre Dame reopening in Paris
item.description: Zelenskyy said his first face-to-face encounter with Trump since the election was “good and productive,” adding they had agreed to continue working together.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: The famed Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris reopens to great fanfare
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Notre Dame is reopening its doors for the first time since a fire in 2019 nearly destroyed Paris' beloved 12th-century cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral reopens its doors for first time since devastating 2019 fire
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, WFLA, NBC
